title: Direct identification from thiglycolate by MALDI-TOF
steps: Once turbidity was detected in thioglycolate broth, extract 1.5 mL with a Pasteur pipette and place them on an Eppendorf tube.
Add 50 µL of Triton 10%, mix with a vortex and centrifuged (13200 rpm/2 minutes).
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 500 µL of distilled water and re-centrifuged in the same conditions.
Discard the supernatant, and resuspend the resulting pellet in 50 µL of absolute ethanol. Vortex briefly.
Spot 1 µL onto a MALDI-TOF target plate and let it dry.
Add 1µL of formic acid and let it dry.
Overlaid with 1µL MALDI matrix (α-cyano-4-hydroxycinnamic acid) for mass spectrometry.
creator: Fátima Galán-Sánchez
